rationale: The query/hyp engine is a probability-generating-function analysis of a configuration-model-like ensemble (arbitrary incidence/degree distribution, local tree approximation) used to derive a phase transition threshold and the size of an emergent giant component/core plus distance/neighborhood moments; the candidate instead analyzes a biased random-walk (Markov) process on a fixed-degree-distribution network, deriving stationary occupation probabilities and mean transit times — a transport-dynamics calculation, not a generating-function derivation of a structural giant-component/core threshold, so the machinery does not cohere despite shared "arbitrary degree distribution" language. The candidate's object is also random complex networks with arbitrary degree distributions studied for routing/navigation — the same network-science substrate as the query itself (not the hypothetical's constraint-satisfaction/SAT-instance target), so no genuine cross-field transfer has occurred.
